The Maker

J.M. Amoedo

Custom knife maker and leatherworker. Every piece built from raw materials, finished by hand.

The Story

The Story Behind
the Blades

J.M. Amoedo is a custom knife maker and leatherworker driven by a deep respect for the craft of working with raw materials. Every piece that leaves the workshop is built from the ground up, shaped by hand, and tested before it goes out the door.

The blade work starts at the forge — raw steel drawn out under heat and hammer, then ground to geometry, heat-treated and hand-finished. Handles are fitted from natural materials: hardwoods, stabilized burls, bone, horn and brass, each chosen for the knife and the person who will carry it.

The leatherwork follows the same philosophy — oil-tanned hides, saddle-stitched by hand, built to age beautifully and last a lifetime.

The goal with every piece is the same: something that feels right, performs well, and gets better with use.

Craft & Philosophy

Materials & Method

Steel

High-carbon and stainless steels selected for hardness, toughness, edge retention and corrosion resistance — matched to the knife's intended purpose.

Handles & Leather

Stabilized hardwoods, natural burls, bone, horn, antler and brass for handles. Full-grain and oil-tanned hides for leather goods — chosen for how they look, age and feel.

Finishing

Each blade is hand-sanded through progressive grits, heat-treated to specification, and sharpened before leaving the workshop. Leather goods are burnished, conditioned and saddle-stitched.

The Work

Recent Pieces

Commission Your Piece

Knife or leather — get in touch to discuss what you have in mind.
